examples.title: Selection Sort Ascending
examples.description: Basic Selection Sort algorithm for sorting in ascending order
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SelectionSort(A[0..n-1])
  FOR i FROM 0 TO n-2 DO
    min_index ← i
    FOR j FROM i+1 TO n-1 DO
      IF A[j] < A[min_index] THEN
        min_index ← j
      END IF
    END FOR
    IF min_index ≠ i THEN
      SWAP A[i] AND A[min_index]
    END IF
  END FOR
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Outer loop selects position, inner loop finds minimum in remaining unsorted portion, then swap if needed. Each pass places one element in final position.

examples.title: Selection Sort in C
examples.description: Complete C implementation with user input and display functions
examples.explanation: Complete C program demonstrating Selection Sort. The algorithm finds minimum element in each pass and swaps it with the first unsorted position. Time: O(n²), Space: O(1)

examples.title: Selection Sort in Python
examples.description: Python implementation with optional trace showing array after each pass
examples.explanation: Python version with optional trace feature. Uses tuple unpacking for swapping. Identical O(n²) complexity but cleaner syntax.

examples.title: Selection Sort in Java
examples.description: Java implementation as a reusable method with main for testing
examples.explanation: Java implementation with O(n²) time and O(1) space. Uses traditional swap with temporary variable. Demonstrates in-place sorting principle.
